# Fujin UI Toolset — Full Reference
> Version: 0.1.0 | Platform: Web (Mantine v7) + Native (planned)
> This file is intended for agent consumption. Read llms.txt first for a quick index.

---

## 1. DESIGN RULES

### Sharp-Edge Mandate
All components must use `borderRadius: tokens.radius.default` which equals `0`.
This applies to every element including Mantine component API props (e.g., `<Menu radius={tokens.radius.default}>`).
Mantine's internal popover wrappers may apply their own radius — always override via the component's `radius` prop, not just inline styles.

### Token-First Rule
Every design value (color, spacing, radius, typography, shadow, transition) must be sourced from `tokens.json`.
Import pattern: `import tokens from '../tokens.json';`
Never hardcode hex values, px values, or font names directly in components.

### Progressive Disclosure Rule
- Cards with >2 actions: overflow into `ActionMenu`, expose max 2 primary actions
- Workflows with >3 steps: use `WorkflowStepper`
- Cards with secondary detail: use `DataCard` with the `detail` prop + `Collapse`

### Density Rule
The UI must be invisible — it stays out of the way of data.
Use whitespace from the spacing scale to separate control groups.
Avoid stacked borders (double-border anti-pattern).

---

## 2. TOKEN CONTRACT

File: `tokens.json`

### Color Palette — Slate
```
slate[0]  #f8fafc   (lightest)
slate[1]  #f1f5f9
slate[2]  #e2e8f0
slate[3]  #cbd5e1
slate[4]  #94a3b8
slate[5]  #64748b
slate[6]  #475569
slate[7]  #334155
slate[8]  #1e293b
slate[9]  #0f172a   (darkest)
```

### Semantic Colors
```
background.base      #0f172a   — page background
background.surface   #1e293b   — cards, panels
background.elevated  #334155   — dropdowns, tooltips
background.overlay   #475569   — modals

text.primary         #f8fafc   — headings, labels
text.secondary       #cbd5e1   — body text
text.muted           #94a3b8   — placeholders, hints
text.inverse         #0f172a   — text on light backgrounds

border.subtle        #334155   — dividers, card borders
border.default       #475569   — input borders
border.strong        #94a3b8   — focus rings, emphasis

interactive.default  #64748b   — primary action background
interactive.hover    #94a3b8
interactive.active   #cbd5e1
interactive.disabled #334155

status.danger        #f87171
status.warning       #fbbf24
status.success       #4ade80
status.info          #60a5fa
```

### Spacing
```
base    4    (base unit — use for gaps inside components when xxs is too tight)
xxs     2    (micro gaps: label→value, icon→text, tightly stacked items)
xs      10
sm      12
md      16   (standard padding)
lg      20
xl      24
xxl     32
```
Usage: `tokens.spacing.scale.md` returns `16` (number). Append `px` for CSS strings.
`tokens.spacing.base` returns `4` (number). `tokens.spacing.scale.xxs` returns `2` (number).

**When to use micro spacing:**
- `gap: tokens.spacing.scale.xxs` — icon-to-text pairs, label-above-value stacks, tight inline badges
- `gap: tokens.spacing.base`      — between items within a control group (e.g. rows in a validation list)
- `gap: tokens.spacing.scale.xs`  — between control groups within a section

### Typography
```
fontFamily.base  "Verdana", -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, "Segoe UI", Roboto, Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif
fontFamily.mono  "JetBrains Mono", "Fira Code", "Cascadia Code", Menlo, Consolas, monospace

fontSize.xs   10
fontSize.sm   12
fontSize.md   14
fontSize.lg   16
fontSize.xl   20
fontSize.xxl  24
fontSize.h1   32  through  fontSize.h6  12

fontWeight.regular   400
fontWeight.medium    500
fontWeight.semibold  600
fontWeight.bold      700

lineHeight.tight    1.2
lineHeight.base     1.5
lineHeight.relaxed  1.75

letterSpacing.tight    -0.02em
letterSpacing.base      0em
letterSpacing.wide      0.05em   (used on uppercase labels)
letterSpacing.widest    0.1em
```

### Radius
```
All sizes → 0
tokens.radius.default = 0
```

### CSS Custom Properties — Runtime Reference
All vars below are injected by `FujinThemeProvider` and switch automatically on theme
toggle. Use these in every inline style object — never use raw hex values.

var(--fujin-bg-base)               page/app background
var(--fujin-bg-surface)            cards, panels, rail
var(--fujin-bg-elevated)           dropdowns, tooltips, active nav item background
var(--fujin-bg-overlay)            modals, overlays

var(--fujin-text-primary)          headings, labels, active item text
var(--fujin-text-secondary)        body text, inactive nav items
var(--fujin-text-muted)            placeholders, hints, icon color
var(--fujin-text-inverse)          text on light backgrounds

var(--fujin-border-subtle)         dividers, card borders
var(--fujin-border-default)        input borders
var(--fujin-border-strong)         focus rings, emphasis

var(--fujin-interactive-default)   primary action/button background
var(--fujin-interactive-hover)     hovered interactive state
var(--fujin-interactive-active)    pressed/active interactive state
var(--fujin-interactive-disabled)  disabled interactive state

var(--fujin-status-danger)         error, destructive action
var(--fujin-status-warning)        caution
var(--fujin-status-success)        positive outcome
var(--fujin-status-info)           informational

var(--fujin-shadow-sm)             subtle elevation
var(--fujin-shadow-md)             card/panel elevation
var(--fujin-shadow-lg)             modal/overlay elevation

### Opacity
tokens.opacity.disabled  0.5   — disabled controls, unavailable actions
tokens.opacity.loading   0.7   — loading/in-progress state

---

## 3. COMPONENT API REFERENCE

---

### ToolShell
Layout shell with collapsible left rail navigation.

```tsx
import { ToolShell, type NavItem } from './components';

interface NavItem {
  icon:     ReactNode;
  label:    string;
  active?:  boolean;
  onClick?: () => void;
}

interface ToolShellProps {
  navItems:  NavItem[];
  logo?:     ReactNode;   // shown at top of rail; click toggles collapse
  footer?:   ReactNode;   // shown at bottom of rail (user profile, settings)
  children:  ReactNode;   // main content area
  header?:   (controls: { toggleMobile: () => void; mobileOpen: boolean }) => ReactNode;
  // render prop — if provided, an AppShell.Header (48px) is rendered above the rail.
  // Use to place a burger button or top bar content. controls.toggleMobile opens the
  // mobile rail; controls.mobileOpen reflects current state.
}
```

Behavior:
- Rail expands (220px, icon + label) / collapses (60px, icon only) via logo click
- Collapsed: labels appear as `Tooltip` on hover
- Mobile: rail hidden by default; pass a `header` render prop to render a top bar with
  a burger button. The render prop receives `{ toggleMobile, mobileOpen }`.
- `active` prop on NavItem highlights the current route

Mobile usage — burger button wired to header render prop:

```tsx
import { Burger } from '@mantine/core';
import { ToolShell } from './components';

<ToolShell
  navItems={navItems}
  logo={logo}
  header={({ toggleMobile, mobileOpen }) => (
    <Burger
      opened={mobileOpen}
      onClick={toggleMobile}
      hiddenFrom="sm"
      color="var(--fujin-text-muted)"
      size="sm"
    />
  )}
>
  {children}
</ToolShell>
```

- `hiddenFrom="sm"` hides the burger on desktop (Mantine responsive utility prop — no custom CSS needed)
- `color="var(--fujin-text-muted)"` passes the CSS custom property directly to Mantine's Burger line color
- If Mantine does not accept a CSS var in the `color` prop, replace with an `UnstyledButton` wrapping three `<div>` bars styled with `background: 'var(--fujin-text-muted)'`
- The header bar container (`AppShell.Header`) already has `display: flex; align-items: center` — the Burger renders left-aligned by default; add additional header content (title, actions) as siblings to the right

---

### DataCard
Card with progressive disclosure and action overflow.

```tsx
import { DataCard, type CardAction } from './components';

interface CardAction {
  label:     string;
  icon?:     ReactNode;
  onClick:   () => void;
  danger?:   boolean;
  disabled?: boolean;
}

interface DataCardProps {
  title:        string;
  badge?:       ReactNode;    // right side of header (e.g., StatusBadge)
  children:     ReactNode;    // always-visible primary content
  detail?:      ReactNode;    // hidden until user expands (useDisclosure)
  actions?:     CardAction[]; // first 2 shown; rest in overflow ActionMenu
  defaultOpen?: boolean;      // disclosure open on mount (default: false)
}
```

Behavior:
- If `detail` is provided, a toggle button appears below primary content
- Max 2 primary actions; overflow goes into a `Menu` triggered by "More ▾"
- No border on the disclosed detail panel — the toggle button acts as the divider

---

### WorkflowStepper
Multi-step workflow with per-step validation and navigation.

```tsx
import { WorkflowStepper, type WorkflowStep } from './components';

interface WorkflowStep {
  label:        string;
  description?: string;
  content:      ReactNode;
  validate?:    () => true | string;  // true = pass, string = error message
}

interface WorkflowStepperProps {
  steps:           WorkflowStep[];
  onComplete?:     () => void;        // fires when final step is completed
  allowStepClick?: boolean;           // allow clicking back to prior steps (default: false)
}
```

Behavior:
- `validate()` on the current step is called before advancing
- Error string is displayed below the stepper in `status.danger` color
- `allowStepClick` only permits navigating to already-completed steps (not forward)
- Final step shows "Complete" instead of "Next →"
- After completion, `Stepper.Completed` renders and navigation is hidden

---

### FormShell
Form layout wrapper. Does not own form state — caller uses Mantine `useForm`.

```tsx
import { FormShell, type FormShellProps } from './components';

interface FormShellProps {
  onSubmit:     (e: FormEvent<HTMLFormElement>) => void;
  children:     ReactNode;    // form fields (Mantine inputs)
  submitLabel?: string;       // default: 'Submit'
  loading?:     boolean;      // disables submit, shows Loader
  error?:       string;       // form-level error (below fields, above footer)
  actions?:     ReactNode;    // secondary actions (e.g., Cancel) — left-aligned
}
```

Usage:
```tsx
const form = useForm({ initialValues: { name: '' } });

<FormShell
  onSubmit={form.onSubmit(handleSubmit)}
  submitLabel="Save"
  loading={saving}
  error={serverError}
  actions={<CancelButton />}
>
  <TextInput label="Name" {...form.getInputProps('name')} />
</FormShell>
```

Behavior:
- `loading` prevents double-submit (form `onSubmit` is swallowed while loading)
- Form-level `error` is separate from field-level errors (which Mantine handles)
- `actions` slot is left-aligned; submit button is always right-aligned

---

### Mantine Checkbox (pattern — not a Fujin component)
Mantine's `Checkbox` is the correct input for boolean form fields. Use it instead of a
native `<input type="checkbox">`. Three things are required for Fujin compliance:

1. `radius={tokens.radius.default}` — sets the visual indicator to sharp-edge
2. CSS custom property `--checkbox-color` — overrides the checked fill color
3. `styles.label` — typography tokens on the label

Mantine v7 Checkbox exposes `--checkbox-color` and `--checkbox-radius` as CSS custom
properties on the root element. Set them via `styles.root` cast to `React.CSSProperties`.

```tsx
import { Checkbox } from '@mantine/core';
import tokens from '@tokens';

<Checkbox
  label="Enabled"
  checked={value}
  onChange={e => setValue(e.currentTarget.checked)}
  radius={tokens.radius.default}
  styles={{
    root: {
      '--checkbox-color':  'var(--fujin-interactive-active)',
      '--checkbox-radius': '0px',
    } as React.CSSProperties,
    label: {
      fontFamily: tokens.typography.fontFamily.base,
      fontSize:   tokens.typography.fontSize.sm,
      color:      'var(--fujin-text-secondary)',
      cursor:     'pointer',
    },
    input: {
      cursor:      'pointer',
      borderColor: 'var(--fujin-border-default)',
    },
  }}
/>
```

Notes:
- Do **not** use `accentColor` — Mantine Checkbox renders a custom visual indicator, not a
  native checkbox. `accentColor` on `styles.input` has no effect.
- The native `<input type="checkbox">` is hidden by Mantine; `styles.input` controls
  the hidden input's border (shown on focus ring) and cursor.
- `--checkbox-color` accepts any CSS color string including `var(--fujin-*)` values.

---

### StatusBadge
Inline status indicator. Text only, no icons.

```tsx
import { StatusBadge, type StatusBadgeProps } from './components';

interface StatusBadgeProps {
  status: 'success' | 'danger' | 'warning' | 'info' | 'neutral';
  label:  string;
  size?:  'sm' | 'md';  // default: 'sm'
}
```

Color mapping:
- `success`  → `tokens.color.semantic.status.success`
- `danger`   → `tokens.color.semantic.status.danger`
- `warning`  → `tokens.color.semantic.status.warning`
- `info`     → `tokens.color.semantic.status.info`
- `neutral`  → `tokens.color.semantic.text.muted`

Rendered as a `<span>` with border matching the status color. Uppercase, wide letter-spacing.

---

### ActionMenu
Standalone overflow menu. Trigger is a ⋯ button.

```tsx
import { ActionMenu, type ActionMenuItem } from './components';

interface ActionMenuItem {
  label:     string;
  icon?:     ReactNode;
  onClick:   () => void;
  danger?:   boolean;
  disabled?: boolean;
}

interface ActionMenuProps {
  items: ActionMenuItem[];
}
```

Behavior:
- Danger items render in `status.danger` color
- Disabled items have `opacity: 0.5` and swallowed `onClick`
- Dropdown positioned `bottom-end`, `radius={tokens.radius.default}`
- Use inside `DataCard` overflow slot or standalone on any surface

---

### SectionHeader
Consistent section/panel header. Renders as a `<div>` with bottom border.

```tsx
import { SectionHeader, type SectionHeaderProps } from './components';

interface SectionHeaderProps {
  title:        string;
  description?: string;   // subtitle below title, muted color
  action?:      ReactNode; // right-side slot (button, badge, menu)
}
```

Note: `SectionHeader` does not own its bottom margin — the parent controls spacing below it.

---

### FujinToastProvider
Imperative toast notification system. Renders via React portal into `document.body`.

```tsx
import { FujinToastProvider, useToast } from './components';
import type { ToastOptions } from './components';

// Wire once at app root — inside FujinThemeProvider so CSS vars are available:
<FujinThemeProvider>
  <FujinToastProvider>
    <App />
  </FujinToastProvider>
</FujinThemeProvider>

// In any component inside the provider:
const { show } = useToast();

show({ status: 'success', title: 'Done',   message: 'Operation completed.' });
show({ status: 'danger',                   message: 'Something went wrong.' });
show({ status: 'warning', title: 'Notice', message: 'Check your settings.', duration: 6000 });
show({ status: 'info',                     message: 'Persists until dismissed.', duration: false });
```

```tsx
interface ToastOptions {
  message:   string;
  status:    'success' | 'danger' | 'warning' | 'info';
  title?:    string;
  duration?: number | false;  // ms; default 4000; false = persist until × dismissed
}
```

Behavior:
- Toasts stack bottom-right, column-reverse (newest at bottom, older pushed up)
- Max 5 toasts — oldest trimmed when limit exceeded
- Left border accent matches status color (`var(--fujin-status-*)`)
- Enter animation: opacity + translateY on mount
- Auto-dismiss after `duration` ms; `false` = persistent until close button clicked
- `useToast()` throws if called outside `FujinToastProvider`

---

## 3.5. PAGE COMPOSITION

### Provider Setup — wire once at app root
Both providers are required. `MantineProvider` is Mantine's base; `FujinThemeProvider`
injects the CSS custom properties.

```tsx
import { MantineProvider } from '@mantine/core';
import { FujinThemeProvider } from './components';

function Root() {
  return (
    <MantineProvider>
      <FujinThemeProvider defaultMode="dark">
        <App />
      </FujinThemeProvider>
    </MantineProvider>
  );
}
```

### Full page example
Demonstrates: ToolShell wiring, multi-section layout, spacing ownership,
SectionHeader + content pattern, DataCard overflow actions, DataTable with row actions.

```tsx
import { Box, Burger, UnstyledButton } from '@mantine/core';
import {
  ToolShell, SectionHeader, DataCard, DataTable,
  StatusBadge, ActionMenu,
} from './components';
import type { NavItem, DataColumn } from './components';
import tokens from './tokens.json';

const navItems: NavItem[] = [
  { label: 'Dashboard', icon: '⊞', active: true, onClick: () => {} },
  { label: 'Reports',   icon: '≡', onClick: () => {} },
  { label: 'Settings',  icon: '⚙', onClick: () => {} },
];

type Job = { id: string; name: string; status: string; created: string };

const columns: DataColumn<Job>[] = [
  { key: 'id',      label: 'ID',      sortable: true },
  { key: 'name',    label: 'Name',    sortable: true },
  { key: 'status',  label: 'Status',
    render: (row) => <StatusBadge status={row.status as any} label={row.status} /> },
  { key: 'created', label: 'Created', sortable: true },
];

export function App() {
  return (
    <ToolShell
      navItems={navItems}
      logo={
        <span style={{
          fontFamily: tokens.typography.fontFamily.base,
          fontWeight: tokens.typography.fontWeight.bold,
          color: 'var(--fujin-text-primary)',
        }}>
          Acme
        </span>
      }
      footer={
        <UnstyledButton style={{
          fontFamily: tokens.typography.fontFamily.base,
          fontSize:   tokens.typography.fontSize.sm,
          color:      'var(--fujin-text-muted)',
          cursor:     'pointer',
        }}>
          Sign out
        </UnstyledButton>
      }
      header={({ toggleMobile, mobileOpen }) => (
        <Burger
          opened={mobileOpen}
          onClick={toggleMobile}
          hiddenFrom="sm"
          color="var(--fujin-text-muted)"
          size="sm"
        />
      )}
    >
      {/* Caller owns all layout spacing — use a single Box with gap */}
      <Box style={{
        padding:       tokens.spacing.scale.xl,
        display:       'flex',
        flexDirection: 'column',
        gap:           tokens.spacing.scale.xl,
      }}>

        {/* Section pattern: SectionHeader owns no margin — caller adds marginTop to content */}
        <Box>
          <SectionHeader
            title="Active Jobs"
            description="Last 24 hours"
            action={
              <UnstyledButton style={{
                fontFamily: tokens.typography.fontFamily.base,
                fontSize:   tokens.typography.fontSize.sm,
                color:      'var(--fujin-text-primary)',
                cursor:     'pointer',
              }}>
                + New Job
              </UnstyledButton>
            }
          />
          <Box style={{ marginTop: tokens.spacing.scale.md }}>
            <DataCard
              title="Job #1042"
              badge={<StatusBadge status="success" label="Running" />}
              actions={[
                { label: 'Restart',  onClick: () => {} },
                { label: 'Logs',     onClick: () => {} },
                { label: 'Edit',     onClick: () => {} },
                { label: 'Delete',   danger: true, onClick: () => {} },
              ]}
            >
              <span style={{
                fontFamily: tokens.typography.fontFamily.base,
                fontSize:   tokens.typography.fontSize.sm,
                color:      'var(--fujin-text-secondary)',
              }}>
                Target: Production DB — 1,240,500 rows processed
              </span>
            </DataCard>
          </Box>
        </Box>

        <Box>
          <SectionHeader title="All Jobs" />
          <Box style={{ marginTop: tokens.spacing.scale.md }}>
            <DataTable
              columns={columns}
              rows={rows}
              rowKey="id"
              pageSize={20}
              rowActions={(row) => (
                <ActionMenu items={[
                  { label: 'Edit',   onClick: () => {} },
                  { label: 'Delete', danger: true, onClick: () => {} },
                ]} />
              )}
            />
          </Box>
        </Box>

      </Box>
    </ToolShell>
  );
}
```

### Spacing ownership rules (summary)
- The outer `Box` owns `padding` for the page content area
- Sections are separated by `gap` on the outer `Box` — not by `marginBottom` on sections
- Within a section, `marginTop` on the content `Box` (not `marginBottom` on `SectionHeader`)
  separates the header from its content — because `SectionHeader` does not own its margins
- No component sets its own external margins — all spacing is the caller's responsibility

---

## 4. AGENT INTEGRATION NOTES

### For agents implementing Mantine UI using this toolset:

1. Read this file (`llms-full.txt`) in full before writing any component. It contains the complete token contract, CSS var reference, and page composition patterns. You do not need to open `tokens.json` directly.
2. Do not introduce a Mantine theme override unless a specific friction point requires it. Document the reason.
3. If a new component is needed, check if an existing component covers it via composition before creating a new one.
4. All new components must follow the same token-import pattern as existing ones.
5. Flag any hardcoded value as a violation — it must be resolved before the component is accepted.
6. The `radius: 0` rule has no exceptions unless explicitly approved.
7. Do not add `marginBottom` or external spacing to component containers — callers own layout spacing.

### G31P (Gemini) Offload Protocol:
- Low-complexity, mechanical, clearly-scoped components → brief to G31P
- Judgment-heavy (disclosure logic, layout shells, workflow behavior) → Fujin only
- All G31P output is reviewed by Fujin before acceptance
- Brief must include: file path, token import pattern, props interface, behavior spec

---

---

### DataTable
Sortable, paginated data table with loading and empty states. Generic over row type.

```tsx
import { DataTable, type DataColumn, type SortDirection } from './components';

interface DataColumn<T> {
  key:       keyof T & string;
  label:     string;
  sortable?: boolean;
  width?:    number | string;
  render?:   (row: T) => ReactNode;  // custom cell renderer; receives full row
}

interface DataTableProps<T extends Record<string, unknown>> {
  columns:       DataColumn<T>[];
  rows:          T[];
  rowKey:        keyof T & string;   // unique identifier field
  loading?:      boolean;
  emptyMessage?: string;             // default: 'No records found.'
  pageSize?:     number;             // omit for no pagination
  rowActions?:   (row: T) => ReactNode; // right-aligned per-row slot (use ActionMenu)
  onSortChange?: (key: keyof T & string, direction: SortDirection) => void;
}
```

Behavior:
- Sort is client-side by default (internal state). If `onSortChange` is provided, the callback fires and the caller is responsible for passing pre-sorted rows back.
- Pagination is internal — slices rows by `pageSize` and current page. Row count label shown left of pagination controls.
- `loading` with no rows: shows centered `Loader` in tbody
- `loading` with existing rows (reload): rows shown at 50% opacity during fetch
- Empty state: single full-width row with `emptyMessage`
- Sort indicators: ↕ (unsorted), ↑ (asc), ↓ (desc) appended to header label
- Last column reserved for `rowActions` slot (48px wide, right-aligned)

Usage:
```tsx
<DataTable
  columns={[
    { key: 'name',   label: 'Name',   sortable: true },
    { key: 'status', label: 'Status', render: (row) => <StatusBadge status={row.status} label={row.status} /> },
    { key: 'date',   label: 'Date',   sortable: true },
  ]}
  rows={data}
  rowKey="id"
  loading={fetching}
  pageSize={20}
  emptyMessage="No results match your filter."
  rowActions={(row) => <ActionMenu items={actionsFor(row)} />}
/>
```

---

## 5. KNOWN GAPS (Planned)
- ~~Data table~~ Done
- ~~Light theme semantic layer~~ Done — CSS custom properties via FujinThemeProvider; both themes active
- Component-level token tier (for variant-heavy components) — Layer 3
- React Native primitive set (consumes same tokens.json) — Layer 3
